tldr: Matthew Brennan won the final stage from a reduced sprint after a late kangaroo crossing crashed the lead group — his first WorldTour stage win, on the day Vine sealed the overall.

narrative.headline: Brennan wins stage 5, with a kangaroo in the script
narrative.body:
169.8 km, eight laps of a Stirling circuit, 3,436 m of elevation, finishing on the 2 km Mount Barker Road climb. The finale produced the week's most-shared video: a kangaroo crossed the road and caused a crash in the lead group. The race still resolved in a reduced sprint.

Brennan attacked over the top of the leadout. Kwiako led out at 300 m, Gilmore countered, Brennan took the line. A first WorldTour stage win, 21 years old, for the rider who'd then go on to lead Van Aert into Arenberg at Roubaix three months later.

gcImpact.headline: Vine wins the GC
gcImpact.body: Vine the overall winner, Schmid 2nd at 1:03, Sweeny 3rd at 1:12. Brennan keeps the young rider classification.

riderPerformances.name: Matthew Brennan
riderPerformances.narrative: First WorldTour stage win, attacking over the top of the lead-out on the Mount Barker Road kicker into Stirling. With the young rider classification already locked, the stage win was the season's first major statement.

crashes.description: Kangaroo crossed into the lead group inside the closing kilometres, causing a crash. No GC impact.
